MicroGPT Under a Microscope

Watch a Model Learn to Predict Text

A small neural network trains on one sentence. Watch its predictions shift from random noise to accurate next-word prediction.

Target Sentence
Model Output — Predicted Next Word at Each Position
Step 0
Loss (NLL)
—
Lower = less surprised by the correct word
Correct
0/8
Top prediction matches actual next word
Loss Curve —

Each row shows how a position distributes its attention across earlier positions. Darker = more weight. Upper-right is masked (no looking ahead).

A modular map of all 720 weights in the transformer network. Each cell shows a single parameter's value over time. Blue means positive, Red means negative.

1. Input Embeddings

Token Embeddings (wte) 9 tokens × 8 dims
Position Embeddings (wpe) 8 positions × 8 dims

2. Causal Self-Attention Layer

Query Weights (wq) 8 × 8
Key Weights (wk) 8 × 8
Value Weights (wv) 8 × 8
Output Projection (wo) 8 × 8

3. Feedforward Layer (MLP)

FC1 (Expansion) 16 hidden × 8 inputs
FC2 (Projection) 8 outputs × 16 hidden

4. Language Model Head

LM Head Weights 9 vocab × 8 inputs
Output at Each Checkpoint
